records.rationale: Rekon now emits semantic.scopeResolution from structured evidence about the transform's identifier-rewriting scope classifier. The buggy revision identifies SwitchStatement as an unmodeled lexical boundary, while the fixed classifier includes it and leaves the switch discriminant outside that scope; ordinary switch statements remain outside this emitter's jurisdiction.

Direct source review confirms that the RSC hoist transform collected reference names and called find_owner for each name from one scope anchor, which could not distinguish fully shadowed local occurrences from outer bindings. Rekon emits semantic.scopeResolution on that name-only mechanism and stays silent after the transform resolves concrete identifier occurrences to declaring scopes; upstream shadowing tests verify when values must and must not be captured.

Rekon emits semantic.cleanupCompleteness from structured lifecycle-wait evidence in both affected close paths. Independent judgment confirms the sequential buggy path and retains the aggregate path for verification, while both all-settled fixed paths remain clean.

Rekon now emits semantic.cleanupCompleteness from source-matched fail-fast lifecycle evidence in all three affected shutdown-hook implementations. Independent judgment retains every buggy path, while the all-settled and guarded fixed implementations remain clean.

Rekon now emits semantic.dependencyResolution from current source-matched evidence on the buggy revision. The candidate identifies the later-match overwrite path, remains a semantic claim pending independent judgment, and is silent after the first-match fix.

Direct source review confirms that resolvePerContext iterated distinct instance links but its static branch called the token-wide get method without the current link, returning the last registered provider for every item. Rekon emits semantic.dependencyResolution on that guarded bypass; returning wrapperRef.instance removes the evidence, and upstream unit and integration tests verify three distinct providers are returned in order.

Rekon now joins structured request/reply retention on connection-owned state with explicit releases across a complete EvidenceGraph. The buggy Fastify revision retains the cross-file resource-lifetime claim for autonomous verification, while the response-boundary release makes the fixed revision clean.
Rekon now emits events.inverseListenerDelegation only on the buggy revision. The assessment identifies the off-to-on contract mismatch directly and remains silent after the upstream fix.

Rekon now emits semantic.cacheIntegrity from current source-matched evidence on the buggy compiled-code read/write path. The upstream defect is absent from fix-related lines after integrity verification is added; a separate JSON sidecar candidate remains an independent assessment rather than being hidden by the benchmark.

Direct source review confirms that the buggy dispatcher constructs AbortError with params.reason only as cause while explicitly selecting its generic default message. Rekon emits semantic.errorPropagation on that changed constructor; passing the reason as the message removes the structured evidence in the fixed revision, and the upstream click and assertion tests verify the reason reaches API errors and call logs.

Direct source review confirms that read() resolves its Promise from zipfile entry events and rejects stream and not-found failures, but the buggy revision has no zipfile error listener. Rekon emits semantic.errorPropagation from that incomplete Promise-to-emitter bridge and clears after the fixed revision forwards zipfile errors through toExtractError into the same reject channel.

Rekon now emits semantic.optionPropagation from source-matched callback-backed overrides informed by structured spread, override, fallback, and callback context. Independent judgment retains both buggy publish paths as verification-required when imported contracts remain external, while both fixed nullish-fallback paths remain clean.

Direct source review confirms that ManifestPlugin and ProgressPlugin used logical OR to combine explicit boolean options with visible true defaults, coercing false back to true. Rekon emits semantic.optionPropagation for each same-property truthy default on the buggy revision and stays silent after presence-aware merging; the upstream regression test verifies that false remains observable for all affected ProgressPlugin options.

Rekon now emits validation.partialAllowlistMatch only on the buggy revision. The assessment is constrained to a named allowlist used in an explicit whole-value rejection branch and remains silent once the regex is anchored.

Rekon now emits semantic.errorPropagation from structured throw guards and error identities plus source-matched downstream handling. Independent judgment retains the merged condition/abort identity on the buggy revision, while the fixed revision with separate ConditionError and AbortError branches remains clean.

Rekon emits semantic.resourceLifetime from structured evidence that a request-scoped closure is attached to a connection-lived socket inside the request's socket callback. Moving the timeout listener to the request removes the cross-lifetime retention evidence in the fixed revision.

Rekon emits semantic.cacheIntegrity from structured evidence that getPackageMetadata memoizes by package identity while a version-specific result branch depends on version outside the key. Independent judgment retains the buggy cross-call contract as verification-required, while separating disk and network caches removes the evidence in the fixed revision.

Direct source review confirms that the Fastly store assigned an async load directly to a Promise-named member and returned that member to later callers without clearing it after rejection. Rekon emits semantic.cacheIntegrity on that retained rejected Promise and clears after the fixed revision identity-guards a rejection handler that resets the active cache entry; upstream tests verify retry after transient failure and reuse after success.

Direct source review confirms that XHR instrumentation registered a named readystatechange handler, used readyState === 4 as its terminal completion branch, and never detached the same handler. Rekon emits semantic.resourceLifetime on that one-shot listener retention and clears after the fixed revision removes it after completion reporting; upstream tests verify removal at state 4 and preserve completion behavior and the onreadystatechange proxy path.

Direct source review confirms that normalizeFetchInput constructed a temporary Request from the caller input and init, spread the caller init into returned options, then replaced its signal with request.signal. Rekon emits semantic.optionPropagation on that derived-signal handoff and clears when the fixed revision forwards the caller-owned signal; upstream tests verify exact signal identity and cancellation of an in-flight body read.

Direct source review confirms that useDocHandles started Promise.allSettled inside a dependency-bearing React effect and called the useState-derived setHandleMap from its continuation without returning cleanup. Rekon emits semantic.cleanupCompleteness on that superseded-effect path and clears after the fixed revision invalidates the old continuation during cleanup; the upstream regression rerenders before the first lookup resolves and verifies the stale URL set cannot overwrite the current map.

Direct source review confirms that Babel's renameVisitor skipped child scopes when bindingIdentifierEquals failed and already requeued method keys and decorators, but did not requeue a SwitchStatement discriminant evaluated in the parent scope. Rekon emits semantic.scopeResolution on that omitted traversal exception and clears after the fixed revision queues path.get("discriminant"); upstream fixtures cover identifier, member, binary, and call-expression discriminants under case-local shadowing.

Direct source review confirms resolveTargetIdFromTabs used one first-match lookup across raw targetId, suggestedTargetId, tabId, and label namespaces, then returned the selected tab's canonical target ID even though the same resolver already rejected ambiguous prefix matches. Rekon emits semantic.dependencyResolution on that cross-namespace path and clears after the fixed revision gathers distinct canonical target IDs and rejects multiple exact matches; upstream tests cover each friendly namespace colliding with another tab's raw ID and route behavior returns 409 without acting.

Direct source review confirms makeCacheKey normalized opts.path to fullPath with a slash fallback, then passed the raw optional member into pathHasQueryOrFragment before serializing and returning fullPath as the key path. Rekon emits semantic.cacheIntegrity on that raw-versus-normalized guard and clears when the guard uses fullPath; the upstream regression covers query parameters with an omitted path.

Direct source review confirms createPhasesTask already modeled teardown dependencies but scheduled teardown projects through the same default Dispatcher phase as ordinary work, while the dispatcher stopped immediately after maxFailures. Rekon emits semantic.cleanupCompleteness on that teardown-aware scheduler without an interruption exemption and clears when teardown projects are isolated in ignoreMaxFailures phases; the upstream regression proves teardown still runs after the failure limit.

Direct source review confirms getImportCompletionAction passed an explicit moduleSymbol into getAllReExportingModules for every source, allowing a re-export candidate to replace a bare ambient module. Rekon emits semantic.dependencyResolution on that unconditional explicit-source expansion and clears when bare specifiers use direct symbol export information; the upstream fourslash regression verifies the ambient module specifier.

Direct source review confirms addConsumeAwareSignal set the cancelled flag from context.signal, but fetchPage responded with zero-argument Promise.reject and discarded the signal reason. Rekon emits semantic.errorPropagation on that signal-backed empty rejection and clears when context.signal.reason is forwarded; the upstream regression verifies the cancellation reason reaches observers.

Direct source review confirms prepareRolldownOptimizerRun spread rolldownOptions.transform before assigning ESBUILD_BASELINE_WIDELY_AVAILABLE_TARGET, so JavaScript object precedence overwrote a caller target. Rekon emits semantic.optionPropagation on that default-after-user-options assignment and clears when the default moves before the spread; the upstream issue provides the failing configuration and expected target behavior.

Direct source review confirms withCancel attached an abort listener closing over reject while normal Promise resolution and rejection paths left the listener installed. Rekon emits semantic.resourceLifetime on that listener-with-settlement shape and clears when every normal and exceptional path removes the named handler; the upstream fix documents the retained closure and preserves once-only abort handling.

Direct source review confirms isRefIdentifier excluded noncomputed method and static property keys but not noncomputed PropertyDefinition keys, causing the mock import walker to classify class field names as value references. Rekon emits semantic.scopeResolution on that missing adjacent reference-position rule and clears when the classifier returns true only for the field initializer; the upstream regression covers import injection around class properties.

Direct source review confirms determineStaleAt converted max-age=0 and s-maxage=0 to undefined, so CacheHandler returned before storing validator-backed responses. Rekon emits semantic.cacheIntegrity on that zero-freshness omission and clears when validators permit a zero stale time plus bounded retention; upstream tests verify no-cache and max-age=0 responses issue conditional requests and reuse 304 responses.

Direct source review confirms AndroidDevice._send retained Promise continuations in this._callbacks while _close shut down connections, driver, and backend without settling that map. Rekon emits semantic.cleanupCompleteness on the undrained callback collection and clears when _close rejects every pending callback and clears the map; the upstream reproduction identifies indefinitely pending commands after close.

Direct source review confirms computeModuleSpecifiers admitted a candidate when either the imported file was outside node_modules or the candidate path was inside node_modules, without distinguishing auto-import ranking. Rekon emits semantic.dependencyResolution on that guard and clears when auto-import mode admits the local monorepo path; the upstream fourslash regression verifies the local relative specifier is selected.

Direct source review confirms compressed response adapters rejected new Error objects built from the original message while retry policy separately depended on ECONNRESET. Rekon emits semantic.errorPropagation on that code-losing stream wrapper and clears when network errors are rejected unchanged; upstream tests verify retries for both immediate and mid-stream compressed connection resets.

Direct source review confirms both Vitest configuration paths forced preTransformRequests=false while browser-mode signals were available but did not guard the assignments. Rekon emits semantic.optionPropagation on both buggy paths and clears when the Node default is applied only outside browser mode; upstream tests verify browser values remain unset while Node values remain false.

Direct source review confirms PlaywrightServer enumerated and launched browsers through its Playwright owner, but close only stopped the WebSocket server. Rekon emits semantic.resourceLifetime on that owner-versus-transport shutdown mismatch and clears when close iterates and closes every owned browser; the upstream CI failure tied leaked Edge processes to worker teardown timeouts.

Direct source review confirms transformBlockScopedVariable injected undefined initialization for loop-body declarations while excluding all loop heads, including an uninitialized for head nested inside an outer loop. Rekon emits semantic.scopeResolution on the missing nested-for exception and clears when nested for-statement initializers are included; upstream direct and indirect fixtures verify each outer iteration executes the inner loop.
